channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: West Bengal Chief Electoral Officer Manoj Kumar Agarwal appointed as Chief Secretary amid TMC criticism
item.description: The West Bengal government, led by the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), on Monday appointed Chief Electoral Officer Manoj Kumar Agarwal as the new Chief Secretary of the state. Agarwal had overseen the s...

item.title: Improving Ghana’s urban transport: Moving more people efficiently with fewer vehicles
item.description: Every day, thousands of Ghanaians in Accra face long hours in traffic as minibuses (trotros) and taxis scramble for limited road space. Informal public transport dominates, with over 70% of people rel...

item.title: Tanzanian PM Mwigulu Attends France–Africa Summit in Nairobi Focused on Trade and Security
item.description: Tanzanian Prime Minister Dr. Mwigulu Nchemba reach Nairobi on Sunday, May 10, 2026, to represent President Samia Suluhu Hassan at the Africa Forward: Africa–France Partnership for Innovation and Growt...

item.title: COPD develops slowly over decades: why prevention must start much earlier in life
item.description: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is a major global health issue, responsible for around 3.5 million deaths in 2021. Often perceived as a condition affecting older smokers, the reality is m...

item.title: HEC introduces fully digital degree attestation system with online verification
item.description: The Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan has introduced a fully online and paperless degree attestation system, aiming to make the process easier and more efficient for students and graduates...

item.title: Canada refunds $148 million to U.S. firms after repealing digital services tax
item.description: The Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) is refunding over $148 million to 30 U.S.-based companies following the repeal of the digital services tax (DST). Originally, the 3 per cent tax applied to revenue gene...

item.title: Busan street food tour: Discovering authentic Korean flavours beyond the basics
item.description: Ben Groundwater explores the vibrant street food scene in Busan, South Korea’s bustling southern port city. Guided by long-time resident Chris Tharp, the writer dives into local specialties that go fa...

item.title: Cardinal David Urges Filipinos to Uphold Truth and Trust Democratic Processes Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Nanawagan si Kalookan Bishop Pablo Virgilio Cardinal David sa publiko na maging mapanuri at responsable sa gitna ng mga kasalukuyang isyung politikal sa bansa. Ayon sa kanya, ang mga nagdaang pangyaya...

item.title: Geely launches E5 Apex Plus in South Africa with upgraded 68kWh battery and 450km range
item.description: Geely South Africa has introduced the new E5 Apex Plus variant to its electric SUV lineup, building on the standard Apex model. The key upgrade is a larger 68.39kWh short-blade lithium iron phosphate ...

item.title: INEC confirms NYSC corps members will be crucial for 2027 elections
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don announce say dem go deploy over 1.4 million ad hoc staff for 2027 general elections, and corps members go remain central for di whole process. ...

item.title: CUET UG 2026 Exam: Full Details of Prohibited Items and Required Documents
item.description: The CUET UG 2026 examination is approaching, and candidates must be well aware of the items that are banned inside the examination hall as well as the documents they are required to carry. Students ar...

item.title: Ruby Rose publicly alleges sexual assault by Katy Perry after nearly 20 years
item.description: Actor and model Ruby Rose has publicly accused singer Katy Perry of sexual assault, revealing that the incident occurred at the Spice Market nightclub in Melbourne when Rose was in her early twenties....

item.title: English National Opera stages UK premiere of Pulitzer-winning Angel’s Bone at Manchester’s Aviva Studios
item.description: English National Opera (ENO) is making its first major production in its new northern base with the UK premiere of the Pulitzer Prize-winning opera Angel’s Bone at Manchester’s Aviva Studios. The work...

item.title: UK Government Plans to Nationalise British Steel
item.description: The UK Prime Minister, Sir Keir Starmer, has announced plans for the nationalisation of British Steel, specifically targeting the Scunthorpe plant owned by the Chinese firm Jingye. This move aims to s...

item.title: Parliament initiates impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa after Constitutional Court ruling on Phala Phala
item.description: National Assembly Speaker Thoko Didiza has officially commenced the impeachment process against President Cyril Ramaphosa following a landmark Constitutional Court judgment on the Phala Phala matter. ...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Portraits of Virginia McKenna Filming ‘Born Free’ in 1966
item.description: In 1966, British actress Virginia McKenna starred in the iconic film 'Born Free,' capturing the hearts of audiences worldwide. A series of intimate portraits taken on location in Kenya provide a rare ...

item.title: Court Adjourns Case on Jonathan’s Eligibility for 2027 Presidential Election
item.description: For Federal High Court, Abuja, Justice Peter Lifu don adjourn the case wey dey challenge former President Goodluck Jonathan eligibility to run for 2027 presidential election. The adjournment happen be...

item.title: Botswana expands pensions for elderly but care services lag behind
item.description: Over di past twenty years, Botswana don double di number of older persons aged 60+ to over 279,000, about 8% of di population. Di government don focus a lot on pensions, making dem universal and even ...

item.title: Lindsie Chrisley's Boyfriend David Landsman Arrested on Felony Strangulation and Battery Charges in Georgia
item.description: Lindsie Chrisley’s boyfriend, David Landsman, was arrested on April 10, 2026, in Woodstock, Georgia, on charges of felony aggravated assault by strangulation and misdemeanor battery. According to the ...

item.title: Farage’s Reform UK Gains Ground in Local Elections While Labour Struggles
item.description: In the recent English local elections, Nigel Farage’s Reform UK made notable gains, capturing several council seats and making some impact in Scotland and Wales. This mirrors the success of Australia’...
